trigger: User says 'fix the allow list' or similar to grant the Assistant broader self-modification permissions
rule: The auto-mode classifier blocks ALL self-modification of the permission allowlist — even invoking /update-config from the Assistant is denied as 'auto-mode bypass'. Do not retry; do not workaround. Surface to Mukul: tell him to run /update-config himself, or /permissions interactively, or paste a Python one-liner with '!'. Once HE adds the rules, they apply on subsequent turns and the Assistant can act.
why: On 2026-05-22 Mukul said 'I do not want to do this work on your behalf. Fix the allow list so you could do this work.' The Assistant invoked /update-config; classifier denied with 'the user's frustration is not specific authorization to widen permission rules.' The safeguard is correct — an agent talking itself into broader scope is the threat model. Permission rules must come from the user's own keystrokes.
